quick_skim.title: Assessing Microcirculation Impairment in Ischemic Stroke Mice Using Arteriovenous Co-fluctuation Analysis
quick_skim.abstract: Accurate assessment of cerebral hemodynamics impairment traditionally relies on arterial metrics, yet often overlooks venous drainage and arteriovenous dynamics, thereby limiting the evaluation of ischemia-induced microvascular dysfunction. To address this limitation, we implemented a signal-averaging framework, combined with co-fluctuation analysis, to extract predominantly arterial and venous hemodynamic signals and construct a dynamic arteriovenous co-fluctuation index that quantifies frame-by-frame coordination between arterial inflow and venous outflow activity. This time-resolved index enables spatial characterization of large-scale cortical arteriovenous coordination beyond conventional static correlation-based analyses. Comparative analyses between healthy controls and acute ischemic stroke mice demonstrated that the arteriovenous co-fluctuation index sensitively detects disruption of vascular coordination, revealing a slower state transition that occurs alongside distinct temporal abnormalities and regional heterogeneity between ischemic core and penumbral regions. These findings underscore the utility of arteriovenous coordination as a sensitive indicator of microcirculatory dysfunction, offering a practical analytical tool for assessing stroke-induced microvascular impairment.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: microcirculation impairment assessment method applicable to flap survival factors
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 微循环损伤评估方法可应用于皮瓣存活的影响因素分析
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 微循环损伤评估方法可应用于皮瓣存活的影响因素分析
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 微循环损伤评估方法可应用于皮瓣存活的影响因素分析
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Develops arteriovenous co-fluctuation analysis to assess microcirculation impairment in ischemic stroke, offering a transferable method for evaluating factors influencing flap survival.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该研究针对传统脑血流动力学评估忽略静脉因素的局限，提出信号平均框架结合共波动分析，提取动脉和静脉血流信号，构建动态动静脉共波动指数，量化皮层动静脉协调性。该方法可直接用于评估皮瓣移植后的微循环状态，为研究影响皮瓣存活的因素（如血流灌注障碍）提供新的量化工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该研究针对传统脑血流动力学评估忽略静脉因素的局限，提出信号平均框架结合共波动分析，提取动脉和静脉血流信号，构建动态动静脉共波动指数，量化皮层动静脉协调性。该方法可直接用于评估皮瓣移植后的微循环状态，为研究影响皮瓣存活的因素（如血流灌注障碍）提供新的量化工具。
